mission.title: capsule: support namespace labeling for tenant owners
mission.description: support namespace labeling for tenant owners. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current capsule deployment
steps.description:
Verify your capsule version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n capsule -l app.kubernetes.io/name=capsule
helm list -n capsule 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working capsule installation.

steps.title: Review capsule configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant capsule configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n capsule -l app.kubernetes.io/name=capsule
kubectl get configmap -n capsule -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=capsule
```
Current capsule and capsule-proxy implementations deny namespace labeling for tenant owners.

steps.title: Apply the fix for support namespace labeling for tenant owners
steps.description:
will add documentation tomorrow :)
```yaml
apiVersion: capsule.clastix.io/v1beta1
kind: Tenant
metadata:
  name: oil
spec:
  owners:
  - name: alice
    kind: User
  namespaceOptions:
    allowedLabels:
     - myorg.net/foo
     - someother.com/bar
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n capsule -l app.kubernetes.io/name=capsule
kubectl get events -n capsule --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "support namespace labeling for tenant owners" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: See the fix PR for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/projectcapsule/capsule/pull/423
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: capsule.clastix.io/v1beta1
kind: Tenant
metadata:
  name: oil
spec:
  owners:
  - name: alice
    kind: User
  namespaceOptions:
    allowedLabels:
     - myorg.net/foo
     - someother.com/bar

apiVersion: capsule.clastix.io/v1beta1
kind: Tenant
metadata:
  name: oil
spec:
  owners:
  - name: alice
    kind: User
  namespaceOptions:
    userLabels:
      allowed:
        - myorg.net/foo
      allowedRegex: ^\w+-lb$
      protected:
        - protected.net/bar
    userAnnotations:
      allowed:
        - myorg.net/foo
      allowedRegex: ^\w+-lb$
      protected:
        - protected.net/bar

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with capsule installed or the issue environment reproducible.
